title: Tax rate type catalogue
description: The 9 tax rate types the module supports. Each entry documents the typical jurisdictions using the type + expected percentage range + applies_to semantics. Consumers of the TaxRateType enum read this catalogue to build UX for the rate-picker widget.

rate_types.description: Consumption tax added at each stage of the supply chain. Predominant in the EU + UK + Canada (GST/HST) + Australia (GST) + many Asian jurisdictions.
Federal-level consumption tax in Canada / Australia / India / Singapore / New Zealand. In Canada, provincial GST can combine with federal GST to form HST.
US-specific consumption tax collected by state + local jurisdictions. Requires nexus tracking per US Wayfair. Rates vary by state (0-10%) + city + zip.
General-purpose consumption tax label used in Japan (10%) + Taiwan (5%) + others. Similar in effect to VAT.

Specific tax on digital services (SaaS, digital ads, marketplace fees). Distinct from VAT — imposed on gross revenue of digital services rather than transaction value. Applies in France (3%), UK (2%), Italy (3%), and others.

Tax withheld at source (typically on cross-border payments to service providers). Applies in many jurisdictions on payments to foreign entities. Not commonly automated — usually calculated per-payout in transaction module.
Tax on specific legal documents (contracts, transfers). Rare in SaaS contexts — mostly applies to physical goods contracts + real-estate. Included for completeness.

Duty imposed on imported goods. Applies to physical shipments crossing borders. Rate varies by HS Code + origin + destination. Not commonly automated in SaaS — included for tenants selling physical merchandise.
Fallback for tax types not fitting the above categories. Tenants who select 'other' must set a descriptive name.

notes: The rate_type enum is the authoritative list. Adding a new type = updating this catalogue + updating the TaxRateType enum.
typical_provider_support is a HINT for tenant admins when picking a provider. The observer's valid_provider_config rule enforces the actual per-provider capability at write time.
